learning_outcomes.description: Demonstrate and apply principles of drug delivery to hypothesise, propose and justify optimum solutions in developing new formulations.
Integrate and apply Good Laboratory Practices (GLP) to a range of formulation concepts in groups.
Integrate evidence from a variety of sources and hypothesise a potential solution using the pharmaceutical sciences to solve a clinical problem.
Create and evaluate a pharmaceutical product using a range of appropriate formulation and analytical methods with a view to optimising the properties of the product.
Justify the formulation design based on literature and experimental data via written and oral modes of communication.

assessments.due_date:
14/08/2026 5:00 pm

Complete the quiz at any time during the 7 days prior to the due date.
assessments.task_description:
The online open-book quiz will assess your learning in Weeks 1 and 2.

  • The quiz will be available for a 7-day period and can be taken at any time during that window.
  • The quiz duration is around 40-45 minutes.
  • The format may be a mixture of question types, for example, multiple choice, multiple answer, matching, ordering, fill in the blank/s, jumbled sentence/s, either/or (e.g., true/false), short answer, and short essay style questions.
  • Questions may be provided randomly from a pool; therefore, each student may receive a different but equivalent quiz.
  • This is an online, open-book quiz undertaken in the venue of your choice.
  • You are required to undertake the quiz without any form of communication with other students.
assessments.submission_guidelines: You may be able to apply for an extension.
assessments.deferral_or_extension: You may be able to apply for an extension.
assessments.late_submission: A penalty of 10% of the maximum possible mark will be deducted per 24 hours from time submission is due for up to 7 days. After 7 days, you will receive a mark of 0.
assessments.additional_info: You may be able to apply for an extension.

assessments.task_description:
This is a group assessment. In this video, you will describe your research plan and why you chose it.

  • The report will be in the form of a pre-recorded video presentation using PowerPoint slides (or equivalent). Submit both the video and a PDF of the slides.
  • All members of the team must contribute to the preparation of the slides and filming of the video.
  • Your camera must be turned on and your face visible during the entire presentation video. Any parts of the presentation that do not comply with this requirement will receive a mark of zero.
  • All members of the team will receive the same mark for this assessment.

 See the course Learn.UQ (Blackboard) site for more details and the marking criteria.
assessments.submission_guidelines: Submit your video file and PDF of the slides via the link provided on the PHRM3202 Learn.UQ site.
assessments.deferral_or_extension: You may be able to apply for an extension.
assessments.late_submission: A penalty of 10% of the maximum possible mark will be deducted per 24 hours from time submission is due for up to 7 days. After 7 days, you will receive a mark of 0.
assessments.additional_info: You may be able to apply for an extension.

assessments.weighting: <dd>20% Submit a final product with a compounding label.  </dd>
assessments.due_date:
8/10/2026 - 22/10/2026

Please see the timetable on the Learn.UQ (Blackboard) site for your group’s due date.
assessments.task_description:
Your team will be assigned to formulate and prepare a suspension formulation of your given case. Your team will also conduct pharmaceutical assays as applicable to the dosage form (e.g. particle size, pH, viscosity content uniformity). Your final product will be submitted for the assessment.

See the course Learn.UQ (Blackboard) site for more details and the marking criteria.
assessments.submission_guidelines: Submission occurs at the end of the 3rd laboratory session for each group.
assessments.deferral_or_extension: You may be able to apply for an extension.
assessments.late_submission: A penalty of 10% of the maximum possible mark will be deducted per 24 hours from time submission is due for up to 7 days. After 7 days, you will receive a mark of 0.
assessments.additional_info: You may be able to apply for an extension.

assessments.task_description:
This is a written report/reflection on your research submitted via Turnitin. This is an individual assessment. You may use the data collected as part of your research in the laboratory sessions.

See the course Learn.UQ (Blackboard) site for more details and the marking criteria.
assessments.submission_guidelines: Submission will be via the Turnitin link provided on Learn.UQ (Blackboard).

learning_activities.topic:
Pharmaceutical research topics: workshops

Attend a 2-hour workshop to discuss and practice questions:

Week 1 - Pharmaceutical research and personalised medicine

Week 2 - Delivery of Biologics & role of research in compounding

Week 3 - Laboratory Research - 1-hour drop-in session

Week 4 - Laboratory Research - 1-hour drop-in session (repeat)


Learning outcomes: L01, L03
